div.p: One cache line fetch = 14 key comparisons (B-tree) vs 3 comparisons (BST)

g.text: Ratio | 4.66x more work | per cache miss

div.p: Implementation Rule: B-trees maximize useful work per cache miss. Nodes should be sized to multiples of the CPU cache line (64B). BST wastes ~70% of bandwidth on pointers/padding.
Total: 14 keys (56B) + 15 ptrs (120B) = 176B Fits in 3 cache lines (192B capacity) 14 binary search comparisons per node access
Total: 3 nodes = 3 comparisons Same memory bandwidth, 4.7x fewer comparisons
